
### checking a VTable call  {.smaller}

    
::::: {.columns}

:::: {.column}

```{.cpp .borderedcodebox}
class A { public:
  virtual void bar() { ... }
};
class B : public A { public:
  void bar() { ... }
};
void example(A *obj) {
  obj->bar();
}
```

::::

:::: {.column .fragment fragment-index=1 .fade-in}

```{.gnuassembler .borderedcodebox .red}
A::bar():
  label $0xe0c5df0b
  ...
B::bar():
  label $0xe0c5df0b
  ...
```

::::

:::::


::::: {.columns}

:::: {.column}

```{.gnuassembler .borderedcodebox}
example:
  // %rax <- vtable address
  movq (%rdi), %rax
  // %rdx <- first vtable entry
  movq (%rax), %rax
  // call using vtable entry
  call *%rax
  ...
```

::::

:::: {.column .fragment fragment-index=1 .fade-in}

```{.gnuassembler .borderedcodebox .red}
example:
  movq (%rdi), %rax
  movq (%rax), %rax
  cmpl $0xe0c5df0b, 2(%rax)
  jne CRASH
  call *%rax
```

::::

:::::

::: {.myoverbox .fragment fragment-index=2 .fade-in-then-out .vlow}

check that we're calling _some_ bar method<br />
still allows calling wrong one

:::
